Q6 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Regarding India’s Ethanol Blending Programme, consider the following statements:
1. India has achieved its 20% ethanol blending target in petrol by 2025.
2. The National Policy on Biofuels (2018), as amended in 2022, advanced the 20% blending target to 2025- 26 from 2030.
3. 1st Generation Ethanol is primarily made from lignocellulosic biomass or agricultural residues.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas announced India’s achievement of 20% Ethanol blending target in petrol by 2025.
• The National Policy on Biofuels (2018), as amended in 2022, advanced the target of 20% blending of ethanol in petrol to 2025- 26 from 2030.
• 1st Generation Ethanol is made from food crops such as cereals, sugarcane, sugar beet, etc.
2nd Generation Ethanol is made from ligno-cellulosic or woody biomass, or agricultural residues.

Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

With reference to the Maratha Military Landscapes of India, recently added to the UNESCO
World Heritage List, consider the following statements:
1. The Maratha Military Landscapes are located exclusively in the state of Maharashtra.
2. The Gingee Hill Fort, located in Tamil Nadu, is one of the sites included in this designation.
3. Raigad Hill Fort, selected by Chhatrapati Shivaji for his permanent capital, is part of this landscape.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• The Maratha Military Landscapes are geographically spread across both Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu.
• The Gingee Hill Fort, located in Tamil Nadu, is explicitly listed as one of the twelve forts within the Maratha Military Landscapes.
• Raigad Hill Fort was selected by Chhatrapati Shivaji as his permanent capital, and it is indeed part of the Maratha Military Landscapes.

Q19 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the sources of Persistent Organic Pollutants (POPs):
1. Elevated temperatures and combustion processes can create POPs.
2. Improper use and disposal of agrochemicals can release POPs into the environment.
3. Unwanted by-products of industrial processes can produce POPs as byproducts.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Elevated temperatures and combustion processes: These can unintentionally create POPs,
such as dioxins and furans, as by-products of incomplete combustion.
• Improper use and disposal of agrochemicals: Certain pesticides, like DDT, are POPs, and
their improper handling can release them into the environment.
• Unwanted by-products of industrial processes: Many industrial activities inadvertently
produce POPs as by-products, which are then released into the environment

Q20 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is not taken into consideration for National Air Quality Index (NAQI)?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Carbon dioxide (CO2) and methane are not taken into consideration for the National Air
Quality Index (NAQI).
• The index focuses on eight primary pollutants, viz. PM10, PM2.5, Nitrogen Dioxide (NO2),
Sulphur Dioxide (SO2), Carbon Monoxide (CO), Ozone (O3), Ammonia (NH3), and Lead (Pb).

Q24 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding Methane emissions:
1. Over a 20-year period, methane is less potent at global warming than carbon dioxide.
2. Methane is one of the primary contributors to the formation of ground-level ozone.
3. Methane remains in the atmosphere for much longer than Carbon dioxide.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Over a 20-year period, methane is 80 times more potent at warming than carbon dioxide,
according to a report by the United Nations Environment Programme.
• Carbon dioxide (CO2) remains in the atmosphere for significantly longer than methane (CH4).
While methane is a much more potent greenhouse gas, its average atmospheric lifetime is
about a decade. In contrast, CO2 can persist in the atmosphere for centuries or even thousands
of years, leading to a long-term increase in its atmospheric concentration.

Q25 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

1. Green hydrogen is produced when natural gas is split into hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
2. Blue hydrogen is produced when hydrogen is produced by splitting water by electrolysis.
3. Brown hydrogen is formed through coal gasification.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Blue hydrogen is produced when natural gas is split into hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
• Green hydrogen is produced when hydrogen is produced by splitting water by electrolysis.
• Brown hydrogen is formed through coal gasification.

2. Random Access Memory (RAM) is a temporary storage that processors use to store programs, while they are running.
3. Primary Memory is non-volatile memory that is permanently built.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Primary memory, also known as main memory or RAM (Random Access Memory), is typically
volatile i.e. it stores data temporarily and loses data when the power is turned off.

Q30 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

WiMax is a wireless communication technology based on

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
WiMax stands for Worldwide Interoperability for Microwave Access. This technology is based
on IEEE 802.16 standard. It is used to provide higher data rates with increased coverage. It is
based on MAN (Metropolitan Area Network) technology and range is up to 50 km.

Q31 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about OSI model:
1. The physical layer is responsible for carrying a raw bit stream over a physical media.
2. The physical link layer provides a flow control mechanism in case data rate absorbed by receiver is less than rate produced at sender.
3. Medium Access Control (MAC) is a sublayer in the physical layer that deals with the issue of
access control to shared channels.
Which of the above statements is/are INCORRECT?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The data link layer consists of two sublayers: the Media Access Control (MAC) sublayer and the
Logical Link Control (LLC) sublayer. The MAC sublayer handles the physical addressing and
manages access to the shared medium, while the LLC sublayer handles flow control, error
checking, and multiplexing protocols. Hence, statements 2 and 3 are not correct.

Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
In star topology, each node is connected directly to a central network hub or switch.

Which of the following statements are true about sequential engineering?

2. Next stage can’t start until the previous stage is finished.
3. Information flow is only in one direction.
4. Reduced product development cost.
5. This method is hardly used today.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Since, the information flow is only in one direction and it is not until the end of the chain that occurs changes and connections can be relayed to the start of the sequence, causing estimated cost to be under predicted.

Q40 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In which of the following decision making model, probability of outcomes are unknown

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Decision under certainty: Each action results in known outcome with probability 1.
Decision under uncertainty: Each outcome has an assigned probability of occurrence.
Decision under risk: Each action results in multiple outcomes, with probability unknown.
Decision under conflict: Outcomes are replaced by course of action determined by opponent
who is trying to maximize his/her objective function.

Q41 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following may result in personal injury and property damage?

 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Accident: Unplanned event resulting in injury and damage.
Incident: Unplanned event that may result is property damage but not involves personal injury.
Near miss: The accident, incident or event that did not happen.
Hazard: Any facility, location, equipment or action having potential of serious injury or death.

Q42 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

What are the various techniques for liquid waste management?
1. Dewatering and sedimentation
2. Incineration
3. Root zone
4. Landfills
5. Composting

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Dewatering/sedimentation: Extraction of water, so solid component left behind, which can be
easily removed.
Incineration: High temperature eliminated any solid component, leaving water free of
hazardous waste like scrap material, stag etc.
Root zone: Liquid waste coming from people (kitchen, bath water) water goes several steps
of processing like sedimentation tank, filter, polishing pond.
Landfills: Dumping of solid waste.
Composting: Transforming solid sediments into organic fertilizer.

Q51 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

During radiography test, which region absorbs less radiation and transmits more.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Lower density region of the component absorbs, less radiation and transmit more. Similarly, if higher density region of the component absorbs more radiation, it transmits less. Thus making the part of film less dark.
Hence, on photographic plate, cracks appear as dark areas and inclusions appear as light region.

Q58 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following contracts is also called K-2 form of contract?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Unit price contract : This is based on estimated quantities of the items involved in the work. The
contractor is obligated to perform the quantities of the work actually required in the field at his
quoted prices, whether they are greater or less than the owner’s estimates. This method is also
called K-2 form of contract.

Q62 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

Which of the following defect arises due to dislocation in material?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Dislocations are linear defects within a crystal's lattice structure where the regular, repeating
arrangement of atoms is disrupted along a line. Thus, dislocations in materials refer to line
defect.

Q63 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

How many kg of sulphur is required per 80 kg of final rubber product to completely cross-link butadiene (C4H6) rubber?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Atomic weight of sulphur = 32
Atomic weight of C4H6 = 12 × 4 + 6 × 1 = 54
Fraction sulphur present in completely crosslinked product = 32÷(32 + 54) = 0.3721
Thus, Amount of sulphur in 80 kg of final rubber = 0.3721 × 80

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Perminvar alloy, having constant permeability is an alloy of

Correct answer: A
Solution

constant magnetic permeability over a wide range of magnetic field strengths.

Q65 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consideration of creep is important in which of following applications?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The slow and continuous elongation of a material with time at constant stress and high temperature
below the elastic limit is called creep. Consideration of creep is crucial in Gas turbine blades
because they operate under continuous, high stress at very high temperatures.

Q69 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The phenomenon that when a current is passed through the junction of two different metals, heat is absorbed or liberated depending upon the direction of current is known as

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The Seebeck effect is the generation of a voltage across the two junctiont made of two dissimilar
conductors when the junctions are kept at a temperature difference, while the Peltier effect is the
absorption or release of heat at the junction of two dissimilar conductors when an electric current
(I) is passed through them.

Q70 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A magnetic material has following characteristics:
1. It gets feebly magnetised in opposite direction of applied magnetic field.
2. Relative permeability, μr is slightly less than 1.
3. In absence of external magnetic field, there are no permanent dipoles in the material.
The nature of magnetic material is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Diamagnetic material properties are:
• No permanent dipoles.
• Gets feebly magnetized in opposite direction of applied magnetic field.
• Relative permeability is slightly less than 1.
Also, the susceptibility of paramagnetic material, ferromagnetic and ferrimagnetic materials is
gretaer than 1 i.e. they are magneitized in the direction of applied magnetic field.

Q72 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which one of the following materials is best suited as electrical insulating material?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Graphite is good electrical conductor.
• Mercury is a goof conductor of electricity and used in thermometer manufacturing and other
chemical applications.
• Tin-antimony alloy is used as soldering materials and is a good conductor.
• Askerel has good electric insulation properties. It is generic term for a group of synthetic,
fire-resistant, chlorinated aromatic hydrocarbons used as electrical insulating liquids in
transformers and capacitors, due to their excellent dielectric properties..

Q73 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the below given statements is not correct regarding soft ferromagnetic materials?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Soft ferromagnetic materials can be easily magnetized and demagnetized. Thus, they have small
value of coercivity i.e. they require a small magnetic field to demagnetize.

Q74 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The property among the given below, which changes in superconducting state of a material is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Specific heat suddenly increases and shows a discontinuous jump when a superconductor transit
from the normal state to the superconducting state. In the supeconducting state, the specific heat
is found to decrease with temperature exponentially. Other mentioned properties remain
unchanged in superconducting state of material.

Q75 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

If |A|≠ 0 for the system AX = 0 ,then which of the following is false?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
In the homogeneous system of equations AX = 0, if |A| ≠ 0 then the system has a unique solution which is the trivial solution i.e. only zero solution.

Q83 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Find out the wrong term in the following series:
2, 3, 4, 4, 6, 8, 9, 12, 16

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The given sequence is a combination of three series:
2, 4, 9, ..................
3, 6, 12, ..................
4, 8, 16, ..................
Each term is twice the preceding term, so 9 is wrong and must be replaced by 8.

Q92 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): In a non-turnkey contracts the work of fixing reinforcement of steel bars can be taken as a work package and can be assigned to one contractor while laying of cement concrete can be assigned to some other contractor as a separate work package.
Statement (II): In non-turnkey contracts, the total project is divided into suitable work packages such that responsibility for poor performance, if any, can be easily attributed to a particular contractor.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
If the work is assigned as given in statement I, it will become difficult to identify the contractor who has not carried out the work as per specifications, if it is found at a later stage that the RCC construction has defects.
Thus, laying of cement concrete and fixing of reinforcement in the form of steel bars of the given
design and specifications are to be treated as one work package.

Q94 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): The Laser printers beams the print onto a metal drum.
Statement (II): Laser printers work by using a heated wire to positively charge a drum, which is then passed over by a laser that reverses the charge in the areas that it hits.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Laser printer works by charging a drum with a heated wire and then using a laser to selectively
discharge areas that represent the image. The toner sticks to discharged areas and is transferred
to paper. Thus, Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true and Statement (II) is the correct
explanation of Statement (I).

Statement (I): An ethical dilemma occurs when a decision is required but different values seem to be in conflict.
Statement (II): In ethical dilemma, the required course of action is clear.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
An ethical dilemma is a type of ethical issue that arises when the available choices and obligations in a specific situation do not allow for an ethical outcome.

Q97 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Inconel contains 80% nickel, 14% chromium and 6% iron
Statement (II): Inconel is a family of austenitic nickel-chromium-based superalloys.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Inconel consists of 80% Nickel, 14% chromium and 6% iron. It is a family of austenitic nickelchromium-
based superalloys. Inconel alloys are oxidation-corrosion resistant materials well suited
for service in extreme environments subjected to pressure and heat.

Solution

Algae, fungi and simple plants are the first organisms to colonize an area in primary succession.

Q100 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).

(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.

Statement (I): Producer’s and consumer’s risk can be defined on the basis of Type-I errors and Type-II errors.
Statement (II): Type-I error is to accept the lot when the true value of the quality characteristic is RQL and Type-II error is associated with the producer’s point to reject when the true value of the quality characteristic is AQL.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Type-II error is to accept the lot when the true value of the quality characteristic is AQL and
Type-I error is associated with the producer’s point to reject when the true value of the quality
characteristic is RQL.
